### Spec
#### Schema
```yaml
bgp:
  advertiseMode: string
  advertisedGroups:
  - string
  advertisedIpRanges:
  - description: string
    range: string
  asn: integer
  keepaliveInterval: integer
description: string
encryptedInterconnectRouter: boolean
networkRef:
  external: string
  name: string
  namespace: string
region: string
resourceID: string
```

<table class="properties responsive">
<thead>
    <tr>
        <th colspan="2">Fields</th>

</thead>
<tbody>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>BGP information specific to this router.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertiseMode</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>User-specified flag to indicate which mode to use for advertisement. Default value: "DEFAULT" Possible values: ["DEFAULT", "CUSTOM"].</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertisedGroups</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>User-specified list of prefix groups to advertise in custom mode.
This field can only be populated if advertiseMode is CUSTOM and
is advertised to all peers of the router. These groups will be
advertised in addition to any specified prefixes. Leave this field
blank to advertise no custom groups.

This enum field has the one valid value: ALL_SUBNETS.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertisedGroups[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertisedIpRanges</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (object)</code></p>
            <p>User-specified list of individual IP ranges to advertise in custom mode. This field can only be populated if advertiseMode is CUSTOM and is advertised to all peers of the router. These IP ranges will be advertised in addition to any specified groups. Leave this field blank to advertise no custom IP ranges.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertisedIpRanges[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertisedIpRanges[].description</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>User-specified description for the IP range.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.advertisedIpRanges[].range</code></p>
            <p><i>Required*</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The IP range to advertise. The value must be a CIDR-formatted string.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.asn</code></p>
            <p><i>Required*</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>Local BGP Autonomous System Number (ASN). Must be an RFC6996 private ASN, either 16-bit or 32-bit. The value will be fixed for this router resource. All VPN tunnels that link to this router will have the same local ASN.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>bgp.keepaliveInterval</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>The interval in seconds between BGP keepalive messages that are sent
to the peer. Hold time is three times the interval at which keepalive
messages are sent, and the hold time is the maximum number of seconds
allowed to elapse between successive keepalive messages that BGP
receives from a peer.

BGP will use the smaller of either the local hold time value or the
peer's hold time value as the hold time for the BGP connection
between the two peers. If set, this value must be between 20 and 60.
The default is 20.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>description</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>An optional description of this resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>encryptedInterconnectRouter</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Indicates if a router is dedicated for use with encrypted VLAN attachments (interconnectAttachments).</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>networkRef</code></p>
            <p><i>Required</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>A reference to the network to which this router belongs.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>networkRef.external</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>A reference to an externally managed ComputeNetwork resource. Should be in the format "projects/{{projectID}}/global/networks/{{networkID}}".</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>networkRef.name</code></p>

## Sample YAML(s)

### Typical Use Case
```yaml
apiVersion: compute.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: ComputeRouter
metadata:
  name: computerouter-sample
spec:
  networkRef:
    name: computerouter-dep
  description: example router description
  region: us-west1
  bgp:
    asn: 64514
    advertiseMode: CUSTOM
    advertisedGroups:
      - ALL_SUBNETS
    advertisedIpRanges:
      - range: "1.2.3.4"
---
apiVersion: compute.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: ComputeNetwork
metadata:
  labels:
    label-one: "value-one"
  name: computerouter-dep
spec:
  routingMode: REGIONAL
  autoCreateSubnetworks: false
```

Note: If you have any trouble with instantiating the resource, refer to <a href="https://docs.cloud.google.com/config-connector/docs/troubleshooting">Troubleshoot Config Connector</a>.
